name: Spanakopita (Greek Spinach Pie)
recipeInstructions:
1. Shred the spinach and mix with scallions, parsley, dill, and salt. Allow to stand for 10 minutes.
2. Squeeze out excess moisture by handfuls.
3. Heat olive oil in a large skillet and sauté onions until transparent (about 5 minutes).
4. Stir in the spinach mixture and cheeses. Remove from heat and cool, stirring occasionally, to room temperature.
5. Beat eggs with a wooden spoon.
6. Brush the bottom and sides of a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish with melted butter. Line the dish with a sheet of filo, pressing the edges firmly into the corners and against the sides of the dish.
7. Brush the surface of the filo with melted butter, spreading it all the way to the outside edges, and lay another sheet of filo on top. Repeat until you have used 7 layers of filo in total.
8. Spread the spinach mixture evenly over the last layer of filo and smooth it into the corners. Place another sheet of filo on top, brush with butter, and repeat, layering with remaining filo.
9. Turn the edges over to form a crust. Brush the top with the remaining butter.
10. Bake at 300 degrees for 1 hour or until the pastry is crisp and delicately browned. Cut into squares and serve hot.
